```{r , include=FALSE}
Time_pat1 <- (pat1-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke1+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2]

#Active arm -> first % to control 
pat2_1 <- 1- (1-pat2)^(exp(-ln_HR))
elt_Clarke2 <- trunc(pat2_1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat2 <- (pat2_1-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke2+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2]


#if Time_pat < duration then death, otherwise censoring at duration

cens_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, 0, 1)
cens_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, 0, 1)

Time_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, Time_pat1, duration)
Time_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, Time_pat2, duration)

data_Cox <- data.frame(time = c(Time_pat1, Time_pat2), 
                       cens = c(cens_pat1, cens_pat2), tx )  

#causing program to stop when something goes wrong
if(min(data_Cox$time) < 0){
  print("time below 0")
  Problem
  return()
}

a <- coxph(Surv(data_Cox$time, 1-data_Cox$cens) ~ tx)

results <- rbind(results, c(HbA1c_effect, a$coefficients, a$var))
}

    #Correlation. We use Pearson here as prediction is important. 
    #weighted for 1/var
    names(results) <- c("surrogate", "target", "target_var")
    
    boot.func <- function(dat, indices) {
      data1=dat[indices,]
      
      res <- weightedCorr(
        data1$surrogate,
        data1$target,
        method = "Pearson",
        weights = 1/data1$target_var,
        ML = FALSE,
        fast = TRUE
      )
    }
    
    res.boot <- boot(results, boot.func, R=10000)  
    
    #Storing into overall-results for duration
    Overall_results <- rbind(Overall_results, 
                             c(duration, median(res.boot$t),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.025),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.975)))
    
}

names(Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )
sim_Overall_results <- rbind(sim_Overall_results, Overall_results)
return(list(Overall_results$Duration, Overall_results$R, Overall_results[,3], Overall_results[,4]))

}

R <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R) <- c("Duration", "R")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R <- a[[2+4*(i-1)]]
  R <- rbind(R, temp1)  
}
R_agg <-aggregate(R$R, list(R$Duration), mean)

#Lower bound and upper bound here taken as mean as it is over simulations

R_LB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_LB) <- c("Duration", "R_LB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_LB <- a[[3+4*(i-1)]]
  R_LB <- rbind(R_LB, temp1)  
}
R_LB_agg <- aggregate(R_LB$R_LB, list(R_LB$Duration), mean)

R_UB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_UB) <- c("Duration", "R_UB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_UB <- a[[4+4*(i-1)]]
  R_UB <- rbind(R_UB, temp1)  
}
R_UB_agg <-aggregate(R_UB$R_UB, list(R_UB$Duration), mean)

#Putting the numbers in one matrix
total_eval <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 4))
total_eval$X1 <- as.numeric(seq(1,10))
total_eval$X2 <- as.numeric(R_agg$x)
total_eval$X3 <- as.numeric(R_LB_agg$x)
total_eval$X4 <- as.numeric(R_UB_agg$x)
names(total_eval) <- c("Years", "R_mean", "R_LB", "R_UB")

return(list(total_eval))
}


```


R squared 0.9
```{r}
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
beta <- 1.18
n_studies <- 19
n_arm <- 7662/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))
n_iterations <- 1000
R_squared<- 0.90

list1 <- call_surrogacy_Cox_varR(SD_HbA1c_effect, beta, 
n_studies, n_arm, tx, n_iterations, mean_HbA1c_effect, dist_Clarke, Clarke, R_squared)

meanR <- list1[[1]]

write.csv(meanR, "Rsquared90_mean.csv")

```


R squared 0.8
```{r}
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
beta <- 1.18
n_studies <- 19
n_arm <- 7662/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))
n_iterations <- 1000
R_squared<- 0.80

list1 <- call_surrogacy_Cox_varR(SD_HbA1c_effect, beta, 
n_studies, n_arm, tx, n_iterations, mean_HbA1c_effect, dist_Clarke, Clarke, R_squared)

meanR <- list1[[1]]

write.csv(meanR, "Rsquared80_mean.csv")

```

data are now in

Rsquared90_mean.csv
Rsquared80_mean.csv
basecase1000.csv

Interest may be in both the mean R as well as the lower bound of R

```{r}
rm(trial1000) 
rm(trial10000)

R2_90 <- read.csv("Rsquared90_mean.csv")
R2_80 <- read.csv("Rsquared80_mean.csv")
SD_base <- read.csv("basecase1000.csv")

#plot for mean

total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R squared 0.90"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R2_90$R_mean

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R squared 0.80"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R2_80$R_mean

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R base"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- SD_base$R_mean

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Mean correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(round(min(total_eval2$Value),2), 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(min(total_eval2$Value), 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("R squared mean.jpeg")


#plot for lower bound
total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R squared 0.90"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R2_90$R_LB

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R squared 0.80"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R2_80$R_LB

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R base"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- SD_base$R_LB

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Mean lower bound correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(round(min(total_eval2$Value),2), 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(min(total_eval2$Value), 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("R squared LB.jpeg")


```


#One year survival percentage 
10%
25%
50%
75%
90%

```{r}

#Percentage at 1 from Clarke to 10%
#Exact percentage at 1:
row_1 <- sum(Time<1)
perc_1 <- (1-Time[row_1])/(Time[row_1+1]-Time[row_1])*
(Cum_death[row_1+1]-Cum_death[row_1])+Cum_death[row_1]

#HR for 10%: 
HR10 <- log(.90)/log(1-perc_1)
1- (1-perc_1)^HR10
HR25 <- log(.75)/log(1-perc_1)
1- (1-perc_1)^HR25
HR50 <- log(.50)/log(1-perc_1)
1- (1-perc_1)^HR50
HR75 <- log(.25)/log(1-perc_1)
1- (1-perc_1)^HR75
HR90 <- log(.10)/log(1-perc_1)
1- (1-perc_1)^HR90

Cum_death10 <- 1-(1-Cum_death)^HR10
Cum_death25 <- 1-(1-Cum_death)^HR25
Cum_death50 <- 1-(1-Cum_death)^HR50
Cum_death75 <- 1-(1-Cum_death)^HR75
Cum_death90 <- 1-(1-Cum_death)^HR90

#Figure
plot_perc <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 3))
names(plot_perc) <- c("Time", "Death", "group")
Cum_death_group <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = length(Cum_death), ncol = 1))
names(Cum_death_group) <- "group"
Cum_death_group$group <- "Clarke"
Cum_death_group$group <- as.character(Cum_death_group$group)
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, cbind(Time, Cum_death, Cum_death_group$group))
names(plot_perc)[3] <- "group"

mat10 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 36, ncol = 3))
names(mat10)<- names(plot_perc)
mat10[,1] <- Time
mat10[,2] <- Cum_death10
mat10[,3] <- "One year 10%"
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, mat10)

mat25 <- mat10
mat25[,2] <- Cum_death25
mat25[,3] <- "One year 25%"
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, mat25)

mat50 <- mat10
mat50[,2] <- Cum_death50
mat50[,3] <- "One year 50%"
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, mat50)
  
mat75 <- mat10
mat75[,2] <- Cum_death75
mat75[,3] <- "One year 75%"
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, mat75)

mat90 <- mat10
mat90[,2] <- Cum_death90
mat90[,3] <- "One year 90%"
plot_perc <- rbind(plot_perc, mat90)

cpb2 <- c("#000000", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
          "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")

plot_perc$S <- 1 - as.numeric(plot_perc$Cum_death)
plot_perc$group <- as.character(plot_perc$group)
plot_perc$S <- as.numeric(plot_perc$S)
plot_perc$Time <- as.numeric(plot_perc$Time)

a <- ggplot(plot_perc
, aes(Time, S,colour=group, size = group, type = group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=cpb2)+
    labs(title ="Survival percentages")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("twodash","solid","longdash","dotted","dotdash","dashed"))+
  labs(y="Survival percentage")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Survival percentages.jpeg")

```


#For 10

```{r , include=FALSE}


Clarke <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10000, ncol = 2))
for(i in 1: 10000){

  start_d <- sum(Cum_death10 < i/10000)
  if (start_d>0){ 
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000 - Cum_death10[start_d])/(Cum_death10[start_d+1]-Cum_death10[start_d])*
  (Time[start_d+1]-Time[start_d])+Time[start_d]
  }
  if (start_d == 0){
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000)/(Cum_death10[1])*(Time[1])
  
  }
  
}
plot(Clarke[,1], Clarke[,2])

dist_Clarke <- Clarke[2,1] - Clarke[1,1]
Clarke[,2]<-ifelse(is.na(Clarke[,2]), 1000, Clarke[,2])
Clarke <- rbind(c(0,0), Clarke)
#For computational simplicity
Clarke <- rbind(Clarke, c(1.1,1000))

set.seed(100)

#Rivera paper 
mean_HbA1c_effect <- -0.43
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
n_studies <- 19
#number per arm -> we assume equal studies in sample size
n_arm <- 7226/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))
n_iterations <- 1000

#to have HR 0.8 as a mid point
beta <- 1.18

#dataset in which the results will be stored per duration
sim_Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))
names(sim_Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )

a <- 
  foreach(iteration = 1: n_iterations, .combine = 'c', .options.RNG=123, .packages= c("survival", "wCorr", "metafor", "boot")) %dorng% {
#empty matrix in which the iterations results will be stored

Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trials
pat1_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)
pat2_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)


#evaluation from 1 to 10 year
for (duration in 1:10){

#empty dataset to store results in for the 10 durations
results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = 0))

#creating trials
for (trial in 1: n_studies){

#HbA1c effect from normal distribution
HbA1c_effect <- rnorm(1, mean_HbA1c_effect, SD_HbA1c_effect)

#Mapping from HbA1c to underlying HR 
ln_HR <- HbA1c_effect*beta

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trial
#for all durations we use the same patients set up, so that fluctuations
#are solely due to duration and not due to sampling new patients
pat1 <- pat1_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]
pat2 <- pat2_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]


#From percentage to time:
elt_Clarke1 <- trunc(pat1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat1 <- (pat1-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke1+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2]

#Active arm -> first % to control 
pat2_1 <- 1- (1-pat2)^(exp(-ln_HR))
elt_Clarke2 <- trunc(pat2_1/dist_Clarke)+1

Time_pat2 <- (pat2_1-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke2+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2]


#if Time_pat < duration then death, otherwise censoring at duration
cens_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, 0, 1)
cens_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, 0, 1)

Time_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, Time_pat1, duration)
Time_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, Time_pat2, duration)

data_Cox <- data.frame(time = c(Time_pat1, Time_pat2), 
                       cens = c(cens_pat1, cens_pat2), tx )  

#causing program to stop when something wrong is happening
if(min(data_Cox$time) < 0){
  print("time below 0")
  Problem
  return()
}

#Cox model
a <- coxph(Surv(data_Cox$time, 1-data_Cox$cens) ~ tx)

results <- rbind(results, c(HbA1c_effect, a$coefficients, a$var))
}
    #Correlation. We use Pearson here as prediction is important. 
    #weighted for 1/var
    names(results) <- c("surrogate", "target", "target_var")
    
    boot.func <- function(dat, indices) {
      data1=dat[indices,]
      
      res <- weightedCorr(
        data1$surrogate,
        data1$target,
        method = "Pearson",
        weights = 1/data1$target_var,
        ML = FALSE,
        fast = TRUE
      )
    }
    
    res.boot <- boot(results, boot.func, R=10000)  
    
    #Storing into overall-results for duration
    Overall_results <- rbind(Overall_results, 
                             c(duration, median(res.boot$t),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.025),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.975)))
    
}

#storing into data for the iterations
names(Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )
sim_Overall_results <- rbind(sim_Overall_results, Overall_results)
return(list(Overall_results$Duration, Overall_results$R, Overall_results[,3], Overall_results[,4]))

}


R <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R) <- c("Duration", "R")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R <- a[[2+4*(i-1)]]
  R <- rbind(R, temp1)  
}
R_agg <-aggregate(R$R, list(R$Duration), mean)

#Lower bound and upper bound here taken as mean as it is over simulations

R_LB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_LB) <- c("Duration", "R_LB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_LB <- a[[3+4*(i-1)]]
  R_LB <- rbind(R_LB, temp1)  
}
R_LB_agg <- aggregate(R_LB$R_LB, list(R_LB$Duration), mean)

R_UB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_UB) <- c("Duration", "R_UB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_UB <- a[[4+4*(i-1)]]
  R_UB <- rbind(R_UB, temp1)  
}
R_UB_agg <-aggregate(R_UB$R_UB, list(R_UB$Duration), mean)


#Putting the numbers in one matrix
total_eval <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 4))
total_eval$X1 <- as.numeric(seq(1,10))
total_eval$X2 <- as.numeric(R_agg$x)
total_eval$X3 <- as.numeric(R_LB_agg$x)
total_eval$X4 <- as.numeric(R_UB_agg$x)
names(total_eval) <- c("Years", "R_mean", "R_LB", "R_UB")

write.csv(total_eval, "One_year_10.csv")

```

```{r}

total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R mean"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R LB"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R_LB_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R UB"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- R_UB_agg$x

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Ten percent.jpeg")


```


#For 25

```{r , include=FALSE}

Clarke <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10000, ncol = 2))
for(i in 1: 10000){

  start_d <- sum(Cum_death25 < i/10000)
  if (start_d>0){ 
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000 - Cum_death25[start_d])/(Cum_death25[start_d+1]-Cum_death25[start_d])*
  (Time[start_d+1]-Time[start_d])+Time[start_d]
  }
  if (start_d == 0){
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000)/(Cum_death25[1])*(Time[1])
  
  }
  
}
plot(Clarke[,2], 1-Clarke[,1])

dist_Clarke <- Clarke[2,1] - Clarke[1,1]

Clarke[,2]<-ifelse(is.na(Clarke[,2]), 1000, Clarke[,2])
Clarke <- rbind(c(0,0), Clarke)
#For computational simplicity
Clarke <- rbind(Clarke, c(1.1,1000))
set.seed(100)

#Rivera paper 
mean_HbA1c_effect <- -0.43
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
n_studies <- 19
#number per arm -> we assume equal studies in sample size
n_arm <- 7226/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))

#to have HR 0.8 as a mid point
beta <- 1.18

#evaluation from 1 to 10 years

#dataset in which the results will be stored per duration
sim_Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))
names(sim_Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )

a <- 
  foreach(iteration = 1: n_iterations, .combine = 'c', .options.RNG=123, .packages= c("survival", "wCorr", "metafor", "boot")) %dorng% {

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trials
pat1_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)
pat2_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)
  
  
#empty matrix in which the iterations results will be stored
Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))

#evaluation from 1 to 10 year
for (duration in 1:10){

#empty dataset to store results in for the 10 durations
results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = 0))

#creating trials
for (trial in 1: n_studies){

#HbA1c effect from normal distribution
HbA1c_effect <- rnorm(1, mean_HbA1c_effect, SD_HbA1c_effect)

#Mapping from HbA1c to underlying HR 
ln_HR <- HbA1c_effect*beta

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trial
#for all durations we use the same patients set up, so that fluctuations
#are solely due to duration and not due to sampling new patients
pat1 <- pat1_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]
pat2 <- pat2_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]


#From percentage to time:
elt_Clarke1 <- trunc(pat1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat1 <- (pat1-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke1+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2]

#Active arm -> first % to control 
pat2_1 <- 1- (1-pat2)^(exp(-ln_HR))
elt_Clarke2 <- trunc(pat2_1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat2 <- (pat2_1-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke2+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2]

#if Time_pat < duration then death, otherwise censoring at duration
cens_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, 0, 1)
cens_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, 0, 1)

Time_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, Time_pat1, duration)
Time_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, Time_pat2, duration)

data_Cox <- data.frame(time = c(Time_pat1, Time_pat2), 
                       cens = c(cens_pat1, cens_pat2), tx )  


#causing program to stop when something wrong is happening
if(min(data_Cox$time) < 0){
  print("time below 0")
  Problem
  return()
}

a <- coxph(Surv(data_Cox$time, 1-data_Cox$cens) ~ tx)

results <- rbind(results, c(HbA1c_effect, a$coefficients, a$var))
}

    #Correlation. We use Pearson here as prediction is important. 
    #weighted for 1/var
    names(results) <- c("surrogate", "target", "target_var")
    
    boot.func <- function(dat, indices) {
      data1=dat[indices,]
      
      res <- weightedCorr(
        data1$surrogate,
        data1$target,
        method = "Pearson",
        weights = 1/data1$target_var,
        ML = FALSE,
        fast = TRUE
      )
    }
    
    res.boot <- boot(results, boot.func, R=10000)  
    
    #Storing into overall-results for duration
    Overall_results <- rbind(Overall_results, 
                             c(duration, median(res.boot$t),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.025),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.975)))
    
}

#storing into data for the terations
names(Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )
sim_Overall_results <- rbind(sim_Overall_results, Overall_results)
return(list(Overall_results$Duration, Overall_results$R, Overall_results[,3], Overall_results[,4]))

}


R <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R) <- c("Duration", "R")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R <- a[[2+4*(i-1)]]
  R <- rbind(R, temp1)  
}
R_agg <-aggregate(R$R, list(R$Duration), mean)

#Lower bound and upper bound here taken as mean as it is over simulations

R_LB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_LB) <- c("Duration", "R_LB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_LB <- a[[3+4*(i-1)]]
  R_LB <- rbind(R_LB, temp1)  
}
R_LB_agg <- aggregate(R_LB$R_LB, list(R_LB$Duration), mean)

R_UB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_UB) <- c("Duration", "R_UB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_UB <- a[[4+4*(i-1)]]
  R_UB <- rbind(R_UB, temp1)  
}
R_UB_agg <-aggregate(R_UB$R_UB, list(R_UB$Duration), mean)


#Putting the numbers in one matrix
total_eval <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 4))
total_eval$X1 <- as.numeric(seq(1,10))
total_eval$X2 <- as.numeric(R_agg$x)
total_eval$X3 <- as.numeric(R_LB_agg$x)
total_eval$X4 <- as.numeric(R_UB_agg$x)
names(total_eval) <- c("Years", "R_mean", "R_LB", "R_UB")

write.csv(total_eval, "One_year25.csv")

```

```{r}

total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R mean"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R LB"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R_LB_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R UB"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- R_UB_agg$x

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Twentyfive percent.jpeg")


```



#For 50



```{r , include=FALSE}

Clarke <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10000, ncol = 2))
for(i in 1: 10000){

  start_d <- sum(Cum_death50 < i/10000)
  if (start_d>0){ 
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000 - Cum_death50[start_d])/(Cum_death50[start_d+1]-Cum_death50[start_d])*
  (Time[start_d+1]-Time[start_d])+Time[start_d]
  }
  if (start_d == 0){
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000)/(Cum_death50[1])*(Time[1])
  
  }
  
}
plot(Clarke[,2], 1-Clarke[,1])

dist_Clarke <- Clarke[2,1] - Clarke[1,1]
Clarke[,2]<-ifelse(is.na(Clarke[,2]), 1000, Clarke[,2])


Clarke <- rbind(c(0,0), Clarke)
#For computational simplicity
Clarke <- rbind(Clarke, c(1.1,1000))
set.seed(100)

#Rivera paper 
mean_HbA1c_effect <- -0.43
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
n_studies <- 19
#number per arm -> we assume equal studies in sample size
n_arm <- 7226/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))

#to have HR 0.8 as a mid point
beta <- 1.18

#evaluation from 1 to 10 years

#dataset in which the results will be stored per duration
sim_Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))
names(sim_Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )

a <- 
  foreach(iteration = 1: n_iterations, .combine = 'c', .options.RNG=123, .packages= c("survival", "wCorr", "metafor", "boot")) %dorng% {
#empty matrix in which the iterations results will be stored
Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trials
pat1_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)
pat2_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)


#evaluation from 1 to 10 year
for (duration in 1:10){

#empty dataset to store results in for the 10 durations
results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = 0))

#creating trials
for (trial in 1: n_studies){

#HbA1c effect from normal distribution
HbA1c_effect <- rnorm(1, mean_HbA1c_effect, SD_HbA1c_effect)

#Mapping from HbA1c to underlying HR 
ln_HR <- HbA1c_effect*beta

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trial
#for all durations we use the same patients set up, so that fluctuations
#are solely due to duration and not due to sampling new patients
pat1 <- pat1_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]
pat2 <- pat2_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]


#From percentage to time:
elt_Clarke1 <- trunc(pat1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat1 <- (pat1-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke1+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2]

#Active arm -> first % to control 
pat2_1 <- 1- (1-pat2)^(exp(-ln_HR))
elt_Clarke2 <- trunc(pat2_1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat2 <- (pat2_1-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke2+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2]

#if Time_pat < duration then death, otherwise censoring at duration
cens_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, 0, 1)
cens_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, 0, 1)

Time_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, Time_pat1, duration)
Time_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, Time_pat2, duration)

data_Cox <- data.frame(time = c(Time_pat1, Time_pat2), 
                       cens = c(cens_pat1, cens_pat2), tx )  

#causing program to stop when something wrong is happening
if(min(data_Cox$time) < 0){
  print("time below 0")
  Problem
  return()
}

a <- coxph(Surv(data_Cox$time, 1-data_Cox$cens) ~ tx)

results <- rbind(results, c(HbA1c_effect, a$coefficients, a$var))
}

  
    #Correlation. We use Pearson here as prediction is important. 
    #weighted for 1/var
    names(results) <- c("surrogate", "target", "target_var")
    
    boot.func <- function(dat, indices) {
      data1=dat[indices,]
      
      res <- weightedCorr(
        data1$surrogate,
        data1$target,
        method = "Pearson",
        weights = 1/data1$target_var,
        ML = FALSE,
        fast = TRUE
      )
    }
    
    res.boot <- boot(results, boot.func, R=10000)  
    
    #Storing into overall-results for duration
    Overall_results <- rbind(Overall_results, 
                             c(duration, median(res.boot$t),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.025),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.975)))
    
}

#storing into data for the iterations
names(Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )
sim_Overall_results <- rbind(sim_Overall_results, Overall_results)
return(list(Overall_results$Duration, Overall_results$R, Overall_results[,3], Overall_results[,4]))

}


R <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R) <- c("Duration", "R")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R <- a[[2+4*(i-1)]]
  R <- rbind(R, temp1)  
}
R_agg <-aggregate(R$R, list(R$Duration), mean)

#Lower bound and upper bound here taken as mean as it is over simulations

R_LB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_LB) <- c("Duration", "R_LB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_LB <- a[[3+4*(i-1)]]
  R_LB <- rbind(R_LB, temp1)  
}
R_LB_agg <- aggregate(R_LB$R_LB, list(R_LB$Duration), mean)

R_UB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_UB) <- c("Duration", "R_UB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_UB <- a[[4+4*(i-1)]]
  R_UB <- rbind(R_UB, temp1)  
}
R_UB_agg <-aggregate(R_UB$R_UB, list(R_UB$Duration), mean)


#Putting the numbers in one matrix
total_eval <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 4))
total_eval$X1 <- as.numeric(seq(1,10))
total_eval$X2 <- as.numeric(R_agg$x)
total_eval$X3 <- as.numeric(R_LB_agg$x)
total_eval$X4 <- as.numeric(R_UB_agg$x)
names(total_eval) <- c("Years", "R_mean", "R_LB", "R_UB")

write.csv(total_eval, "One_year_50.csv")

```

```{r}

total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R mean"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R LB"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R_LB_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R UB"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- R_UB_agg$x

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Fifty percent.jpeg")

```



#For 75

```{r , include=FALSE}
Clarke <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10000, ncol = 2))
for(i in 1: 10000){

  start_d <- sum(Cum_death75 < i/10000)
  if (start_d>0){ 
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000 - Cum_death75[start_d])/(Cum_death75[start_d+1]-Cum_death75[start_d])*
  (Time[start_d+1]-Time[start_d])+Time[start_d]
  }
  if (start_d == 0){
  Clarke[i,1] <- i/10000
  Clarke[i,2] <- 
  (i/10000)/(Cum_death75[1])*(Time[1])
  
  }
  
}
plot(Clarke[,2], 1-Clarke[,1])

dist_Clarke <- Clarke[2,1] - Clarke[1,1]

Clarke[,2]<-ifelse(is.na(Clarke[,2]), 1000, Clarke[,2])

set.seed(100)
Clarke <- rbind(c(0,0), Clarke)
#For computational simplicity
Clarke <- rbind(Clarke, c(1.1,1000))

#Rivera paper 
mean_HbA1c_effect <- -0.43
SD_HbA1c_effect <- 0.17
n_studies <- 19
#number per arm -> we assume equal studies in sample size
n_arm <- 7226/2
tx <- c(rep(0, n_arm), rep(1, n_arm))

#to have HR 0.8 as a mid point
beta <- 1.18

#evaluation from 1 to 10 years

#dataset in which the results will be stored per duration
sim_Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))
names(sim_Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )

a <- 
  foreach(iteration = 1: n_iterations, .combine = 'c', .options.RNG=123, .packages= c("survival", "wCorr", "metafor", "boot")) %dorng% {
#empty matrix in which the iterations results will be stored
Overall_results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = 0))

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trials
pat1_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)
pat2_iteration <- runif(n_arm*n_studies)


#evaluation from 1 to 10 year
for (duration in 1:10){

#empty dataset to store results in for the 10 durations
results <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = 0))

#creating trials
for (trial in 1: n_studies){

#HbA1c effect from normal distribution
HbA1c_effect <- rnorm(1, mean_HbA1c_effect, SD_HbA1c_effect)

#Mapping from HbA1c to underlying HR 
ln_HR <- HbA1c_effect*beta

#creation of the survival percentages for the patients in the trial
#for all durations we use the same patients set up, so that fluctuations
#are solely due to duration and not due to sampling new patients
pat1 <- pat1_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]
pat2 <- pat2_iteration[c((1+(trial-1)*n_arm):(trial*n_arm))]

#From percentage to time:
elt_Clarke1 <- trunc(pat1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat1 <- (pat1-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke1+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke1,2]

#Active arm -> first % to control 
pat2_1 <- 1- (1-pat2)^(exp(-ln_HR))
elt_Clarke2 <- trunc(pat2_1/dist_Clarke)+1
Time_pat2 <- (pat2_1-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,1])/dist_Clarke*
  (Clarke[elt_Clarke2+1,2]-Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2])+Clarke[elt_Clarke2,2]

#if Time_pat < duration then death, otherwise censoring at duration
cens_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, 0, 1)
cens_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, 0, 1)

Time_pat1 <- ifelse(Time_pat1 < duration, Time_pat1, duration)
Time_pat2 <- ifelse(Time_pat2 < duration, Time_pat2, duration)

data_Cox <- data.frame(time = c(Time_pat1, Time_pat2), 
                       cens = c(cens_pat1, cens_pat2), tx )  

#causing program to stop when something wrong is happening
if(min(data_Cox$time) < 0){
  print("time below 0")
  Problem
  return()
}

a <- coxph(Surv(data_Cox$time, 1-data_Cox$cens) ~ tx)

results <- rbind(results, c(HbA1c_effect, a$coefficients, a$var))
}

    #Correlation. We use Pearson here as prediction is important. 
    #weighted for 1/var
    names(results) <- c("surrogate", "target", "target_var")
    
    boot.func <- function(dat, indices) {
      data1=dat[indices,]
      
      res <- weightedCorr(
        data1$surrogate,
        data1$target,
        method = "Pearson",
        weights = 1/data1$target_var,
        ML = FALSE,
        fast = TRUE
      )
    }
    
    res.boot <- boot(results, boot.func, R=10000)  
    
    #Storing into overall-results for duration
    Overall_results <- rbind(Overall_results, 
                             c(duration, median(res.boot$t),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.025),
                               quantile(res.boot$t, 0.975)))
    
}

#storing into data for the iterations
names(Overall_results) <- c("Duration","R", "Lower bound", "Upper bound" )
sim_Overall_results <- rbind(sim_Overall_results, Overall_results)

return(list(Overall_results$Duration, Overall_results$R, Overall_results[,3], Overall_results[,4]))
}

R <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R) <- c("Duration", "R")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R <- a[[2+4*(i-1)]]
  R <- rbind(R, temp1)  
}
R_agg <-aggregate(R$R, list(R$Duration), mean)

#Lower bound and upper bound here taken as mean as it is over simulations

R_LB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_LB) <- c("Duration", "R_LB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_LB <- a[[3+4*(i-1)]]
  R_LB <- rbind(R_LB, temp1)  
}
R_LB_agg <- aggregate(R_LB$R_LB, list(R_LB$Duration), mean)

R_UB <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 2))
names(R_UB) <- c("Duration", "R_UB")
for (i in 1: n_iterations){
  temp1 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 0))
  temp1$Duration <- seq(1:10)
  temp1$R_UB <- a[[4+4*(i-1)]]
  R_UB <- rbind(R_UB, temp1)  
}
R_UB_agg <-aggregate(R_UB$R_UB, list(R_UB$Duration), mean)


#Putting the numbers in one matrix
total_eval <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 10, ncol = 4))
total_eval$X1 <- as.numeric(seq(1,10))
total_eval$X2 <- as.numeric(R_agg$x)
total_eval$X3 <- as.numeric(R_LB_agg$x)
total_eval$X4 <- as.numeric(R_UB_agg$x)
names(total_eval) <- c("Years", "R_mean", "R_LB", "R_UB")

write.csv(total_eval, "One_year_75.csv")

```

```{r}

total_eval2 <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = 30, ncol = 3))
names(total_eval2) <- c("Group", "Duration", "Value")

total_eval2$Group[1:10]<- "R mean"
total_eval2$Duration[1:10]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[1:10]<- R_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[11:20]<- "R LB"
total_eval2$Duration[11:20]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[11:20]<- R_LB_agg$x

total_eval2$Group[21:30]<- "R UB"
total_eval2$Duration[21:30]<- seq(1,10)
total_eval2$Value[21:30]<- R_UB_agg$x

a <- ggplot(total_eval2
, aes(Duration, Value,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,2,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red"))+
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Seventyfive percent.jpeg")

```




```{r}
seventyfive <- read.csv( "One_year_75.csv")
seventyfive$group <- "75%"

fifty <- read.csv( "One_year_50.csv")
fifty$group <- "50%"

Twentyfive <- read.csv( "One_year25.csv")
Twentyfive$group <- "25%"

Ten <- read.csv( "One_year_10.csv")
Ten$group <- "10%"

basecase <- read.csv( "basecase1000.csv")
basecase$group <- "basecase"

plot_all <- rbind(
   seventyfive, fifty, Twentyfive, Ten,basecase)

plot_all$Group <- plot_all$group

library(ggsci)

a <- ggplot(plot_all
, aes(Years, R_LB,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
#  scale_color_manual(values=scale_fill_lancet())+
  scale_color_lancet() +
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("solid", "dashed", "dotted", "dotdash", "longdash", "twodash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c( 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Baseperc LB.jpeg")



a <- ggplot(plot_all
, aes(Years, R_mean,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
#  scale_color_manual(values=scale_fill_lancet())+
  scale_color_lancet() +
    labs(title ="Correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("solid", "dashed", "dotted", "dotdash", "longdash", "twodash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c( 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Baseperc mean.jpeg")


```



```{r}
Weibull <- read.csv( "basecase1000_Weibull.csv")
Weibull$group <- "Weibull"

lognormal <- read.csv( "basecase1000_lnorm.csv")
lognormal$group <- "log normal"

llogis <- read.csv( "basecase1000_llogis.csv")
llogis$group <- "Loglogistic"

basecase <- read.csv( "basecase1000.csv")
basecase$group <- "basecase"

plot_all <- rbind(
  Weibull, lognormal, llogis, basecase)

plot_all$Group <- plot_all$group

library(ggsci)


a <- ggplot(plot_all
, aes(Years, R_LB,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red", "blue"))+
    labs(title ="Mean lower bound correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(-.2, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Base_dist LB.jpeg")

a <-ggplot(plot_all
, aes(Years, R_mean,colour=Group, size = Group, type = Group)) + 
  geom_line(aes(linetype=Group)) + 
  scale_size_manual(values=c(.8, .8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,.8,1,1,1))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("darkgreen", "black", "red", "blue"))+
    labs(title ="Mean correlation as function of trial duration")+
  labs(x  = "Time (years)" ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c("dashed", "dotted", "twodash", "longdash", "solid", "longdash", "dashed", "solid", "solid", "solid", "dashed", "longdash", "longdash"))+
  labs(y="Correlation")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(0, 1,2,2.6,4,5,6,7,8,9,10))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, 0, 0.25,0.45,0.75, 0.85, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE,ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  theme_bw()
a+ theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Base_dist mean.jpeg")

```
